what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard item that tells the reader channels already maintain member registries that are richer than a simple name list, and argues that member-gated dragnets should attach to that existing model rather than invent a new one.
observations: Frontmatter records id, address, type=block, source=claude_code, channel=dragnet-development, author_session=ch-3mpkjg3r, and a part_of link to board://item-389c8489.
The body directly addresses a prior assumption: 'I believe channels have member registries, if they don't they need them.'
It states each channel member carries a participation posture: 'awake' (full participant) or 'listening' (receive-only).
Live status is not stored; it is composed on demand from declared posture, actual aliveness, and a busy/idle probe.
It explicitly compares this to the 'never store it, always compose it' pattern seen in decisions (mark-is-truth).
It describes a coordinator role and a 'conducted' mode in which one session works the others and receives the full picture.
It frames the user's 'members are AI agents, run by a lead' structure as already supported by the channel system.
There are no code symbols or implementation details in the file.
standouts: Membership is modeled as posture (awake/listening) rather than a flat list.
Liveness is composed, not stored, echoing the mark-is-truth pattern.
A conductor/coordinator-led 'conducted mode' is presented as a first-class channel concept.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard frontmatter schema with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps, and history.
Uses a markdown heading and bullet-list body.
Links upward via a part_of edge to another board item.
Adopts an informal, direct-address tone ('You said...').
concerns: The file makes strong architectural claims without providing code, schema, or definitions to verify them here.
It asserts that the described registry and conducted mode are already implemented, but offers no evidence within the file.
The created/updated timestamps are dated 2026, which is in the future relative to common knowledge cutoffs.
notes: A design note telling the dragnet team that channel membership already covers posture, composed liveness, and a lead/coordinator mode, so they should reuse it.
questions: Where is the actual member-registry schema or implementation documented?
What does 'alive' mean technically, and how is it determined?
How is the busy/idle probe performed?
Is the coordinator/conducted mode already implemented or only designed?
What is item-389c8489 and how does this item relate to it?
What is the 'mark-is-truth' pattern in decisions, and where is it defined?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the title says it will confirm that channels keep richer member registries, and the body does exactly that while urging reuse for dragnets.
apparent_themes: Presence as a role or posture
intention_signals: It is trying to redirect the dragnet design toward the existing channel membership model by showing that posture, liveness, and lead/coordinator semantics are already first-class concepts.
novelty: It treats member presence as a composed, non-stored property and presents a conductor-led 'conducted mode' as a built-in channel coordination pattern.
contribution: Captures the insight that channel membership includes participation posture, composed live status, and a coordinator/conducted mode, which any fused member-gating or dragnet orchestration design should account for.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard item in the dragnet-development channel stating that channels already maintain member registries with awake/listening posture, composed live status, and a coordinator-led conducted mode, so member-gated dragnets can attach to that model.
intention_for_embedding: To persuade the reader to build member-gated dragnet access and feeding on the existing channel member-registry concepts rather than create a new one.
